problem: How many different positive integers can be represented as a difference of two distinct members of the set $\{1, 2, 3, \ldots, 14, 15, 16 \}?$

classified_questions.question: What is the maximum positive difference that can be obtained from the set $\{1, 2, 3, \ldots, 14, 15, 16 \}$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can you find the minimum positive difference that can be obtained from the set? If so, what is it?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Based on our observations, what range of positive differences can be achieved by subtracting two distinct members of the set?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can you identify any patterns in the sequence of positive differences?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How does the number of positive differences relate to the size of the set?
classified_questions.label: e

classified_questions.question: Is it possible to obtain all positive integers between 1 and the maximum positive difference? Why or why not?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What would happen if we included negative differences in our calculations? How would that affect the number of different positive integers that can be represented?
classified_questions.label: e
